response:
### 1. Golden Rectangle Calculations

Given:
- Width of the rectangle = 2 meters
- The golden ratio φ = (1 + √5) / 2 ≈ 1.618

#### a. Length of the Rectangle

Using the golden ratio:
$$
\text{Length} = \text{Width} \times \phi = 2 \times \frac{1 + \sqrt{5}}{2} = 1 + \sqrt{5}
$$

#### b. Area of the Rectangle

$$
\text{Area} = \text{Length} \times \text{Width} = (1 + \sqrt{5}) \times 2 = 2(1 + \sqrt{5}) = 2 + 2\sqrt{5}
$$

#### c. Length of the Diagonal

Using the Pythagorean theorem:
$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{\text{Length}^2 + \text{Width}^2} = \sqrt{(1 + \sqrt{5})^2 + 2^2}
$$

$$
(1 + \sqrt{5})^2 = 1 + 2\sqrt{5} + 5 = 6 + 2\sqrt{5}
$$

$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{(6 + 2\sqrt{5}) + 4} = \sqrt{10 + 2\sqrt{5}}
$$

---

### 2. Circle Inscribed in the Golden Rectangle

#### a. Radius of the Inscribed Circle

The largest circle that can fit inside a rectangle touches both the top and bottom sides. Therefore, the diameter of the circle is equal to the width of the rectangle.

$$
\text{Diameter} = \text{Width} = 2 \text{ meters}
$$
$$
\text{Radius} = \frac{\text{Diameter}}{2} = 1 \text{ meter}
$$

#### b. Area of the Region Inside the Rectangle but Outside the Circle

$$
\text{Area of Rectangle} = 2 + 2\sqrt{5}
$$
$$
\text{Area of Circle} = \pi \times \text{Radius}^2 = \pi \times 1^2 = \pi
$$

$$
\text{Area of Region} = \text{Area of Rectangle
